source_name: Code4rena Nudge.xyz Findings and Analysis Report (2025-03) - Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues
issue_title: Missing validation for holdingPeriodInSeconds in NudgePointsCampaigns
summary: NudgePointsCampaigns.createPointsCampaign and createPointsCampaigns validate targetToken but do not require holdingPeriodInSeconds to be greater than zero. A privileged campaign creator can create a points campaign with no effective holding period, undermining the intended token-retention incentive model.
root_cause: missing configuration validation for a core campaign timing parameter
why_not_medium_or_high: the issue depends on privileged campaign creation/configuration and does not by itself show a permissionless exploit path or direct loss of principal assets
operational_risk: campaigns may distribute incentives without enforcing the expected holding behavior, causing inconsistent user expectations and weaker campaign economics
recommended_mitigation: require holdingPeriodInSeconds > 0 when creating points campaigns
emit clear errors for invalid campaign timing configuration
when_it_could_escalate: if an untrusted party can create campaigns
if external systems treat any created campaign as economically valid without admin review
if zero holding period enables reward farming that drains funded incentives
